question_text: Two phone chargers are compared: Charger A outputs 5 V at 2 A, and Charger B outputs 20 V at 0.5 A. Both charge the same phone battery (3.7 V, 2500 mAh) to full capacity. If Charger A is 85% efficient and Charger B is 92% efficient, explain which charger would cost less to operate over one year if used daily, considering a typical electricity cost of 15p per kWh.
correct_answer: Charger B costs less to operate. Charger A costs approximately £0.39 per year while Charger B costs approximately £0.21 per year (answers within ±£0.05 acceptable due to rounding assumptions).
explanation: Calculate input power for each: Charger A: P = 5 × 2 = 10 W; Charger B: P = 20 × 0.5 = 10 W. Both have the same input power, but Charger B is more efficient (92% vs 85%), so it wastes less energy as heat. Over a year: energy = 10 W × 365 days × 24 hours = 87.6 kWh. Charger A useful energy ≈ 74.46 kWh (cost £11.17), Charger B useful energy ≈ 80.59 kWh (cost £12.09). Since the same useful energy reaches the phone, Charger B operating cost is lower because less total energy is drawn from the supply. Note: This advanced question requires understanding that efficiency affects total energy consumption, not just the useful output.
